data.body:
PASS-1 PLAN REVIEW — [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: PASS (no findings).

I assessed the plan's CHOSEN APPROACH against all four G6 dimensions, grounding mechanism-correctness reasoning in the shipped/implemented code (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/{attest,passes,orchestrator}.py) and the closed prerequisite epics (8f6c, e6d9, da27 verified CLOSED with concrete src/rebar/grounding/ + workflow engine + prompt model).

(1) MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — sound. The load-bearing mechanisms hold up, including the edge cases G6 specifically probes:
- TOCTOU / check-then-act (the exact hazard in the G6 checklist): EXPLICITLY raised and resolved in "Design clarifications — CLAIM ATOMICITY (T9)" — the fast signature-verify + open→in_progress are ONE atomic step reusing rebar's existing atomic claim primitive (re-read under lock + optimistic concurrency / ConcurrencyError), NOT a check-then-act sequence. Grounded: attest.claim_gate_check is a pure local HMAC verify + light fingerprint recompute with no LLM/network, fail-closed-when-enabled (unverifiable-material → ok:False), so the atomic claim primitive owns the race.
- Material-edit invalidation data-flow is complete: signature binds the material fingerprint; current_material_fingerprint recomputes from a light read; stale-head AND stale-material both gate; read failure → fail-closed (never wrongly passes).
- Convergence-loop cost mechanism (cap × revisions, prefix-bust each round) is internally consistent and honestly caveated (no cross-run cache saving assumed; per-run cost captured on the sidecar to OBSERVE not benchmark).
- Cap never drops BLOCKING findings (block decision can't be weakened by the advisory cap) — verified in orchestrator (DEFAULT_ADVISORY_CAP, blocking EXEMPT). No hidden ordering/atomicity assumption found.

(2) FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE — solves the named problem (unsound plans executed at open→in_progress), and notably AVOIDS the proxy trap: the cert means "process followed, no red flags + coverage", NOT "perfect" (the round-5 "fail-open hides a hollow signal" critique was examined and resolved with coverage-recorded + sidecar verdicts + client-composed CI predicate).

(3) APPROACH SELECTION — I generated two structurally-different alternatives and judged the chosen approach defensible:
  • (A) inline-blocking-at-claim — the plan weighs this (option A) and rejects it on latency + autonomous-agent confabulation + compound-error grounds; defensibly worse on latency/blast-radius.
  • (B) cache the verdict in a non-signed material-hash sidecar (drop HMAC) — structurally different at the data/signing layer, but loses the composable CI rigor signal (a NAMED requirement) and forgery/replay resistance.
Both are defensibly NOT superior → discarded, not written into the plan (per the anti-priming rule). The plan correctly carries exactly ONE approach (option C, decoupled signed attestation) while documenting the A/B/C weighing in "Design decisions revisited (round 5)".

(4) POSITIVE RATIONALE — present and abundant (why decoupled out-of-band; why advisory-Layer-2 over block-by-default — >30% FP → tool ignored; why deterministic Pass-3; why validity×impact as ONE score; why dep-graph-need ⇒ agent-tier). No rejected-alternatives section is forced (correctly anti-priming).

ANTI-FP honored: this is a genuine design with real choices (not a forced/mechanical change), so alternative-generation was warranted; correctness reasoning was grounded in the actual code via tools, not manufactured.

affirmations: G6 — mechanism correctness (incl. the named TOCTOU/atomicity hazard, explicitly resolved); fitness-for-purpose (solves the problem, not a proxy); approach selection defensible vs reviewer-generated alternatives (no clearly-superior missed option); positive rationale stated for the chosen approach.
